message.content: {"items":[{"title":"ALLBUS 1984 – Allgemeine Bevölkerungsumfrage der Sozialwissenschaften","url_or_doi":"https://www.gesis.org/en/allbus/allbus-home","justification":"Includes German general population data from the mid‑1980s with questions on family formation, fertility intentions, partnership, and related attitudes, which are central to reproductive health context analyses."},{"title":"Family and Fertility Survey in the Federal Republic of Germany (West Germany, early 1980s cohort)","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org","justification":"GESIS hosts German fertility and family‑formation survey datasets from the 1980s that cover timing of births, contraceptive use, union histories, and reproductive intentions in West Germany."},{"title":"German Microcensus Scientific Use Files – 1980s waves","url_or_doi":"https://www.gesis.org/en/microdata/microcensus","justification":"Microcensus SUFs available via GESIS contain large‑scale German population data throughout the 1980s with variables on number of children, marital status, and family structure, enabling reproductive‑health‑related demographic analyses."},{"title":"European Fertility and Family Survey – Germany","url_or_doi":"https://www.gesis.org/en/european-microdata/european-fertility-and-family-survey","justification":"German component of the EFFS hosted by GESIS provides harmonized fertility histories, partnership histories, and contraceptive behavior, relevant for studying reproductive health patterns around the 1980s."},{"title":"German Socio‑Economic Panel (SOEP) – Early Waves via GESIS","url_or_doi":"https://www.gesis.org/en/microdata/soep","justification":"SOEP early waves (starting 1984) include German panel data with detailed household composition, births, pregnancies, and family planning indicators that can be used to analyze reproductive health in the 1980s."},{"title":"Population Policy Acceptance Study (PPAS) – Germany","url_or_doi":"https://www.gesis.org/en/microdata/population-policy-acceptance-study","justification":"PPAS German data stored at GESIS cover fertility preferences, family planning, and attitudes toward demographic and family policies, offering indirect insight into reproductive health concerns in and around the 1980s."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme (ISSP) – Family and Changing Gender Roles: Germany 1988 Module","url_or_doi":"https://www.gesis.org/en/issp/modules/issp-modules-by-topic/family-and-changing-gender-roles","justification":"The 1988 ISSP family module for Germany, archived at GESIS, contains data on family formation, gender roles, and work–family arrangements, which are important contextual factors for reproductive health in late 1980s Germany."},{"title":"Health Surveys – Federal Republic of Germany (1980s waves) archived at GESIS","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org","justification":"GESIS catalog includes German health survey datasets from the 1980s with items on women’s health, maternity, and preventive care utilization that can be used for reproductive‑health‑related analyses."},{"title":"Allied Occupation and Federal Republic Family Surveys – Longitudinal Fertility Histories","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org","justification":"Historical German family surveys with fertility histories, hosted by GESIS, cover cohorts whose reproductive years extend into the 1980s and include variables on births, spacing, and family planning behavior."},{"title":"German Regional Demographic and Health Statistics (Aggregated) – 1980s","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org","justification":"GESIS maintains access to regional demographic datasets for Germany (birth rates, abortion statistics, maternal age at birth) from the 1980s, relevant for macro‑level reproductive health analysis."}]}
